strategy.name: ICT Killzone Sweep + HTF FVG
strategy.description: Fade liquidity sweeps at killzone boundaries when price taps a higher timeframe fair value gap, entering on rejection block formation.

sessions.note: No trades outside of the two New York killzone windows above. London session is excluded.

required.description: Price sweeps above a prior killzone highs or below a prior killzone lows.
After the sweep, price taps into a higher timeframe fair value gap.
Correlated instrument fails to confirm NQ1!'s liquidity sweep, signaling the sweep is a smart money trap rather than a genuine breakout.
required.how_to_check: At the moment NQ1! sweeps its prior high/low, check whether ES1! also sweeps its corresponding prior high/low on the same bar or within the same killzone window.

divergence.bullish_setup: NQ1! sweeps below its prior low BUT ES1! fails to do so, and vice versa. Confirms NQ1! sweep is a liquidity grab.
divergence.bearish_setup: NQ1! sweeps above its prior high BUT ES1! fails to do so, and vice versa. Confirms NQ1! sweep is a liquidity grab.

required.note: SMT divergence is an optional bonus — if both instruments confirm the sweep in the same direction, treat the move as a potential trend continuation and skip the fade setup.

setup_conditions.logic: ALL conditions must be satisfied before looking for entry trigger.

bullish_rejection_block.candle_criteria: Bullish candle close with a large bottom wick.
bullish_rejection_block.limit_placement: 2 ticks above the midpoint of the rejection block candle

bearish_rejection_block.candle_criteria: Bearish candle close with a large top wick.
bearish_rejection_block.limit_placement: 2 ticks below the midpoint of the rejection block candle

liquidity_targets.note: Target the nearest resting liquidity pool above (long) or below (short) price. Use the 1:6 RR as a floor — exit at the liquidity level if it's reached first.

buy_side_liquidity.description: Resting stop-losses above prior highs — target for bearish setups.

levels_in_priority_order.examples: London High, NY Open High, Asia High
Two or more swing highs at the same price (EQH)
Most recent prominent swing high on 15m or 1H

sell_side_liquidity.description: Resting stop-losses below prior lows — target for bullish setups.

levels_in_priority_order.examples: London Low, NY Open Low, Asia Low
Two or more swing lows at the same price (EQL)
Most recent prominent swing low on 15m or 1H

target_selection_rules: Target the nearest buy-side or sell-side level that is at least 1:6 RR from entry.
If no liquidity level satisfies 1:6 RR, skip the trade — do not lower the RR requirement.
If two liquidity levels are equidistant, prefer the session-level over the swing-level.

stop_loss.note: Place stop on the opposite side of the rejection block wick.

rules: Do not enter if price has already closed through the FVG (mitigated) without a rejection.
Cancel limit order if price has already reached into the rejection block zone and reversed without tapping into it.
Cancel limit order if price closes beyond the sweep wick before fill.
